summary: Iteration 2: Tested the new Gadget Store (shop) feature end-to-end. Backend: 13/13 pytest cases pass (product list/filter/search, product detail 200/404, orders auth-gated 401, server-side total computation, malicious client price ignored, empty cart 400, invalid product 400, per-user order scoping). Frontend: full multi-step flow verified — product grid renders 6 seeded products, category (cat-office) and search (shop-search) filters work, product detail navigation, detail-add-to-cart increments cart-badge, cart page shows line items with editable qty and remove, checkout-button says 'Login to checkout' when logged out and redirects to /login, admin login works, checkout form + place-order-button creates order, redirects to /shop/orders?placed=<id>, order-placed-banner and orders-list with correct order-total-<id> render, cart is cleared, and protected routes (/shop/checkout, /shop/orders) redirect to /login when logged out. Existing app features (auth, 30 practice cards, API Playground) not re-tested per request. No new bugs found in the shop feature.
critical_code_review_comments: server.py POST /shop/orders correctly computes total server-side from db.products (client-supplied price is ignored — verified by test_create_order_ignores_client_total). Good.
server.py POST /shop/orders correctly pops both '_id' (bson ObjectId) and 'owner' before returning, so response is JSON-serializable and does not leak internal owner id. Good.
server.py GET /shop/orders is user-scoped by str(user['_id']) — verified isolation between admin and a fresh user in test_orders_are_user_scoped. Good.

server.py currently does not decrement product.stock on order placement. This is fine for a practice-ground app but flag for real e-commerce: over-selling is possible if inventory is not deducted (or oversell allowed even when stock=0). Consider adding a check `if item.qty > product['stock']: raise 400` and an atomic $inc on stock.
CartContext is localStorage-backed and client-only (per spec), so cross-device carts are not synced. Acceptable for the practice scope.

Orders.jsx fetches on mount without a stable loading indicator; tests must wait ~1–2s for [data-testid="orders-list"] to appear (fetch is fast but not instantaneous). Not a bug, but consider adding an aria-live loading state for a11y/testability.
SEED_PRODUCTS runs on every startup via upsert — good idempotent behavior. All 6 products load with expected ids p1..p6.
server.py file is now ~496 lines with auth + playground + shop mixed. Recommend splitting into routers/{auth,playground,shop}.py before it grows further.
test_credentials: Admin: admin@example.com / admin123 (seeded, verified). Fresh users created per test via /api/auth/register with unique emails.
seed_data_creation: Products p1..p6 seeded automatically by seed_products() on backend startup. Test-created orders remain in db.orders (owner-scoped, no cleanup needed — harmless).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Shop feature fully green. Backend regression tests at /app/backend/tests/test_shop.py (13 cases) and /app/backend/tests/backend_test.py (existing 13 auth/playground cases). Run: `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v`. Frontend testids used: product-card-<id>, product-link-<id>, add-to-cart-<id>, detail-add-to-cart, qty-input, cat-<slug>, shop-search, cart-item-<id>, cart-qty-<id>, cart-remove-<id>, cart-total, cart-badge, checkout-button, ship-name/address/city/zip, place-order-button, order-placed-banner, orders-list, order-<id>, order-total-<id>. Login form does NOT use type=email/password inputs — the two inputs are plain <input> under EMAIL and PASSWORD labels; select via `input` order or add data-testid="login-email"/"login-password" if needed. After place-order, wait ~1.5–2s before asserting orders-list because Orders.jsx fetches on mount.
rca_of_the_issue: N/A — no defects found in shop feature. Only observation: Orders.jsx renders 'loading orders…' briefly before the GET /api/shop/orders response arrives; tests should wait for [data-testid="orders-list"] rather than checking immediately after navigation.
